patches.summary: New internal/normalize package: per-Actor dataset-item schema unification. Loads embedded YAML profiles (profiles/*.yaml) for the top newsletter-relevant Actors (apidojo/tweet-scraper, apidojo/twitter-scraper-lite, trudax/reddit-scraper, apify/google-news-scraper, apify/hacker-news-scraper, apify/youtube-scraper, apify/instagram-scraper, apify/smart-article-extractor) plus a default fallback. Each profile maps raw item fields to a unified Item shape (url, title, body, author, published_at, engagement_score, source_actor, hash). Hash is SHA-256 over URL (or raw JSON when no URL) and powers --only-new dedupe. User overrides load from ~/.apify-pp/profiles/.
patches.rationale: Every Apify Actor emits a different item shape; cross-Actor search, novelty diffing, and digest rendering all require one canonical schema. The generator produces per-endpoint typed commands but no normalization layer.

patches.summary: New internal/syncstate package: persists last-sync state to ~/.local/share/apify-pp-cli/sync_state.json (atomic tmp+rename). The run and workflow run commands write a record after they hydrate the local store (last_synced_at, ok flag, items hydrated, token source); the file is consumable by doctor and external tooling without a fresh API call.
patches.rationale: Surfacing when fresh data last landed without re-invoking sync. Pattern adopted from the granola printed CLI's post-print patch set.

patches.summary: Resolved five findings from the library PR's Greptile review: (1) scheduleSpecEqual timezone guard used && where || was needed, masking drift when one side omits the timezone; (2) run --only-new without --wait now fails fast instead of silently emitting an empty list; (3) added the missing FTS5 UPDATE trigger on pp_dataset_items so a direct row UPDATE keeps the search index in sync; (4) ab run launches both Actors concurrently via goroutines + WaitGroup instead of sequentially, for a fair comparison; (5) --max-cost is now enforced for any sample of at least one prior run (was silently bypassed below three), and enforcement was moved out of the --agent/--compact projection-display guard so agent invocations are still capped; a zero-history Actor prints an explicit unenforced-cap warning. Also removed the dead --max-cu flag and gave --offline real fail-fast-on-empty-store behavior.
patches.rationale: Greptile P1/P2 findings on the novel-feature layer plus dead-code cleanup surfaced during pre-publish audit.

patches.summary: New internal/cost package: per-run USD cost estimation (compute units + RAM-GB-hours) plus p50/p90 projection from local run history and a group-by ledger rollup. Powers the `cost report` command, `run`'s pre-flight cost projection, `run --max-cost` budget enforcement, and `ab run`'s cost-per-novel-item judge.
patches.rationale: Apify bills in compute units + RAM + storage + transfer + PPE; the dashboard shows monthly aggregates and the API exposes raw numbers with no per-run rollup. Cost surprise is the most-cited Apify complaint.

patches.summary: New internal/store/extensions.go: pp_-prefixed tables that don't map to a spec endpoint — pp_dataset_items (normalized cross-Actor items) with an FTS5 index + insert/delete triggers, pp_actor_run_history (cost-relevant run metadata), pp_presets (named Actor input presets), pp_workflow_runs (workflow execution log). Exposes EnsureExtensions, UpsertNormalizedItem, HashesSeen, RecordActorRun, LoadActorRunHistory, SavePreset, LoadPreset.
patches.rationale: The generator's store mirrors spec endpoints; the novel features need a normalized item table with full-text search plus run-history and preset persistence.

patches.summary: New `run` command (internal/cli/run.go): runs an Actor, optionally waits/polls to terminal status, fetches + normalizes + persists dataset items, and with --only-new emits only items whose hash is absent from the local store. Prints a p50/p90 cost projection before the run; --max-cost aborts pre-flight when the projection exceeds budget. --format json|markdown|raw. Dataset fetch pages through offset pagination (1000/page, 50-page cap) and path-escapes the dataset ID.
patches.rationale: Novelty diffing (the #1 transcendence feature) and cost-aware runs require local state the generator does not produce.

patches.summary: New `search items` subcommand (internal/cli/search_items.go): FTS5 query over pp_dataset_items, joined to the normalized columns, with --since / --actors / --limit filters. Registered as a child of the generated `search` command via local-variable capture in root.go so the generic resources-FTS `search` is left intact.
patches.rationale: Cross-Actor full-text search has no spec endpoint; it reads the local normalized item store.

patches.summary: New `digest` command (internal/cli/digest.go): pulls topic-matched items from pp_dataset_items, dedupes by URL + title-similarity hash, ranks by engagement+recency+novelty, and fills a Go text/template. Built-in templates default and tiktok-script; --template-file accepts a user template. --offline is always-local (no API). --json emits the structured ranked items.
patches.rationale: Newsletter-ready markdown rendering on top of the local normalized store; nothing in the Apify stack does this.

patches.summary: New `workflow run` subcommand (internal/cli/workflow_run.go): executes a YAML-declared multi-Actor workflow — each step starts an Actor, waits, normalizes + persists items (with optional per-step only_new), then an optional digest block renders to stdout or a file. Records each run to pp_workflow_runs. Registered as a child of the generated `workflow` command.
patches.rationale: Replaces a hand-written orchestration script with one declarative file that an agent can invoke as a single MCP tool.

patches.summary: New `cost` parent + `cost report` subcommand (internal/cli/cost.go): rolls up pp_actor_run_history into a USD ledger grouped by actor, with a --since window and per-actor filter. Read-only (mcp:read-only annotation).
patches.rationale: Per-run cost rollup is derived from locally persisted run history; no equivalent API endpoint.

patches.summary: New `preset` command tree (internal/cli/preset.go): save/list/show/delete named Actor input presets in pp_presets. `preset save` accepts inline/@file input or --from-run (fetches a prior run's input via the API). `run --preset` replays a saved preset. rows.Err() checked after the list query iteration.
patches.rationale: Apify input templates live in Actor READMEs, not the user's workflow; presets persist known-good input JSON locally.

patches.summary: New `ab` parent + `ab run` subcommand (internal/cli/ab.go): runs two Actors with the same input, normalizes both via the shared schema, persists items + run history, and reports items/novel/cost plus overlap percentage. --judge picks the winner: novelty | cost-per-item | cost-per-novel | item-count.
patches.rationale: Head-to-head Actor comparison needs the normalize + novelty layers; impossible from the REST API alone.

patches.summary: New `schedules apply` and `schedules diff` subcommands (internal/cli/schedules_gitops.go): terraform-style declarative schedule management. Reads a YAML schedule file, lists live Apify schedules, computes a create/update/delete plan, and applies creates. Updates/deletes are surfaced as deferred actions in v1 (recreate to update; use the generated `schedules delete` to remove). Registered as children of the generated `schedules` command.
patches.rationale: Apify offers dashboard editing + raw API but no GitOps story; YAML in git makes schedules auditable.

patches.summary: Reworded one SKILL.md prose line that began with the binary name ('apify-pp-cli is the operator CLI...'), which verify-skill parsed as a bash recipe invoking a non-existent `is` command. Changed to 'This CLI is the operator-side interface...'.
patches.rationale: verify-skill scans lines starting with the binary name as command recipes; a descriptive sentence starting with the binary name is a false positive that fails the leg.

patches.summary: Set owner to the slugified contributor name (kevin-magnan) distinct from printer (the GitHub handle kjmagnan1s), and corrected auth_key_url from the spec-derived MDN Authorization-header doc to the real Apify token page (console.apify.com/account#/integrations). Also corrected two novel_features command paths to match the shipped command tree (search -> search items, schedule apply -> schedules apply).
patches.rationale: The generator set owner == printer and pulled auth_key_url from the security scheme's description text; both are corrected to satisfy attribution conventions and give users a working token URL.

deferred_to_upstream.summary: The generator's --since-style duration flags use Go's time.Duration, whose ParseDuration rejects day units ('7d', '30d'). Worked around in the novel commands with a string flag + a parseSinceWindow helper that accepts both Go durations and Nd day forms. The generator should accept day/week forms for time-window flags so hand-written commands don't each re-implement the parser.
deferred_to_upstream.rationale: Every novel command with a time window hit this; a generator-level duration type would remove the per-command workaround.

deferred_to_upstream.summary: An opportunistic auto-refresh of the local store on every non-meta command (PersistentPreRunE, with --no-refresh and APIFY_NO_AUTO_REFRESH opt-outs) was scoped out of this build. The granola printed CLI added the same hook as a post-print patch; it belongs in the generator's standard template.
deferred_to_upstream.rationale: Users should not have to remember to run sync; this is a generator-standard ergonomics feature, not an API-specific one.

deferred_to_upstream.summary: The generator populated auth_key_url from the OpenAPI security scheme's description prose, which for Apify contained an MDN Authorization-header link rather than a token-issuing URL. The generator should prefer a structured key URL or leave the field empty rather than scrape a link out of description text.
deferred_to_upstream.rationale: A wrong key URL ships into doctor's hint and the manifest; corrected by hand here.
